texto: My people? Who are they?I went into the church where the congregationWorshiped my God. Were they my people?I felt no kinship to them as they knelt there.My people! Where are they?I went into the land where I was born,Where men spoke my language.I was a stranger there.“My people,” my soul cried. “Who are my people?”Last night in the rain I met an old manWho spoke a language I do not speak,Which marked him as one who does not know my God.With apologetic smile he offered meThe shelter of his patched umbrella.I met his eyes...And then I knew...
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Rosa Zagnoni Marinoni que provoca temas ligados a motivacao. Ao questionar “My people? Who are they?I went into the church where the congregationWorshiped my God. Were they …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre motivacao, inspiracional, land-where-i-was-born, com foco em boa-noite e inspiracao.
